catalog.title: Speaking · Say it out loud—then shorten it
catalog.description: Say it out loud—then shorten it · A2 speaking in “Transport & city”: Dutch you can use the same week, guided steps, and a short check at the end.

pedagogy.objective: By the end of this lesson, you can use structured speaking frames about transport & city (text-first) to hold a short exchange
can_do_outcomes: Describe a city trip or commute in a few connected sentences
Buy or check-in for train, bus, tram, or metro travel in the Netherlands
Produce a short spoken or text dialogue with polite frames.

grammar_points.point: motion verbs + preposition (naar, met, van … naar …)
examples_nl: Ik ga met de bus naar het station.
We reizen van Utrecht naar Amsterdam.
examples_en: I'm taking the bus to the station.
We're travelling from Utrecht to Amsterdam.

examples_nl: We gaan morgen naar het museum.
Ik ga vanavond uit eten met een vriend.
examples_en: We're going to the museum tomorrow.
I'm going out for dinner with a friend tonight.

grammar_points.point: future with gaan + infinitive for plans
examples_nl: Als de trein vertraging heeft, stuur ik een appje.
Wanneer je incheckt, vergeet niet uit te checken.
examples_en: If the train is delayed, I'll send a text.
When you check in, don't forget to check out.

micro_outcomes: I can recognise key words about transport & city in short Dutch texts or audio.
I can copy at least one useful chunk into a sentence about myself.
I can finish most guided tasks in the lesson without peeking at translations on the second try.
I can pass the quick self-check at the end of the lesson.
I know what to revise tomorrow in two minutes or less.

steps.activity:
Welcome! This lesson is built in small steps so you always know where you are.

**What you'll be able to do:** Use structured speaking frames about transport & city (text-first) to hold a short exchange

**Grammar thread today:** Future plans with gaan + infinitive — you'll get explanations, examples, and production tasks in **Language focus** and **Guided practice**.

Take your time — you can repeat any step. Try to say at least one Dutch sentence out loud today.
steps.teacher_notes: Keep under ~1 min; reassure anxious learners.

steps.activity:
Let's wake up vocabulary about **Transport & city**.

Words you might use today:
• **OV-chipkaart**
• **inchecken**
• **uitchecken**
• **vertraging**
• **spoor**
• **perron**
• ** overstap**
• **fietsenstalling**

**Your turn (1–2 short sentences, Dutch or mixed):**
**Wat deed je gisteren?** — *What did you do yesterday?*

If you only know a few words, that's fine — start there.
steps.example_response: Gisteren nam ik de bus naar het centrum en wisselde ik één keer over. Ik was een beetje te laat, maar ik stuurde een appje: *Sorry, ik ben zo daar.*

illustration.alt: Simple route from A to B with a transfer stop in the middle and OV label

steps.activity:
**Speaking setup (you can stay text-only)**

You are practising short exchanges. Read both roles aloud, then swap.

Rol A/B dialogues (text-first). Gebruik frames: A: Ik heb last van … B: Dan moet je misschien … | A: Moet ik een afspraak maken? B: Ja, bel …

Tip: add **alstublieft** / **dank u wel** in formal situations.

**Today's grammar — future with *gaan***

**Rule:** *gaan + infinitive* plans near future: *Ik **ga** morgen **werken***.

**Patterns:**
• *We gaan zaterdag naar de markt.* · *Ga je vanavond mee?*

**Produce:**
1. Three plans for next week with *gaan*.
2. One negative: *Ik ga niet …* + reason with *want*.

**Pronunciation (listen + repeat)**
**Pronunciation — g / ch:** practise *goed — licht — dag — nacht* with TTS; back *g* vs softer *ch*.

**Language focus — patterns you can reuse**

**motion verbs + preposition (naar, met, van … naar …)**
• NL: Ik ga met de bus naar het station. / We reizen van Utrecht naar Amsterdam.
• EN: I'm taking the bus to the station. / We're travelling from Utrecht to Amsterdam.

**connectors: eerst, daarna, totdat (simple sequences)**
• NL: We gaan morgen naar het museum. / Ik ga vanavond uit eten met een vriend.
• EN: We're going to the museum tomorrow. / I'm going out for dinner with a friend tonight.

**future with gaan + infinitive for plans**
• NL: Als de trein vertraging heeft, stuur ik een appje. / Wanneer je incheckt, vergeet niet uit te checken.
• EN: If the train is delayed, I'll send a text. / When you check in, don't forget to check out.

**Try this:** pick one Dutch sentence above and change only the time or the person (ik → wij / vandaag → morgen), **or** do the “Produce” task from the grammar box above.

**1 — Choral read:** read role A, then B, then swap.

**2 — Slot-fill:** **A:** Mag ik …? **B:** **Natuurlijk**, …

**3 — Add one polite word** to each turn.

**4 — Branch:** B says "Helaas, ik kan niet." — how does A answer kindly?

**5 — Write three lines** at the gemeente desk (text only).

**6 — Opinion:** **Ik vind … leuk, want …** finish it.

**7 — Invite:** suggest a time with **Zullen we … om …?**

**8 — Self-check:** did you use **u** where needed?
---

**All four skills — finish this lesson with these four micro-tasks**

🎧 **Listening:** Use TTS on this Dutch line (slow, then closer to natural): *Ik ga met de bus naar het station.*

📖 **Reading:** Read silently, then say the **main idea in one English sentence**: *Ik ga met de bus naar het station.*

✍️ **Writing:** Write **one new Dutch sentence** that uses **OV-chipkaart** and fits *Future plans with gaan + infinitive*.

🗣️ **Speaking:** Read your new sentence **aloud twice** — exaggerate stress the first time, normal the second.

*(If this lesson was already heavy on one skill, treat that skill as “extra reps” — the point is you always touch all four.)*
steps.teacher_notes: Mix modalities; at least 6 tasks — all concrete above.

steps.activity:
**Speaking task (text is fine — voice optional)**

Write a **6-line** dialogue between you and a neighbour or colleague. Use the frames from step 3 and at least three words from the bank.

**Phrase bank:**
• OV-chipkaart
• inchecken
• uitchecken
• vertraging
• spoor
• perron
•  overstap
• fietsenstalling
• route
• kaartje

**Quick self-check**

Try each question, then tap **Check answer**. The correct answer stays hidden until you submit.

**Tip:** Say Dutch options out loud if that helps.

**Afterwards:** If three or more felt easy, open **Quiz** for a short recap. If not, go back to **Guided practice** once more.
steps.teacher_notes: Rendered with interactive UI when interaction is present.

items.prompt: Pick the best reply when someone says **Hoe gaat het?**
items.feedbackCorrect: Nice — this is a natural, friendly reply.
items.feedbackIncorrect: **Tot ziens** is goodbye; **Graag gedaan** fits after *dank je*. After *Hoe gaat het?* people often say how they feel, e.g. **Goed, dank je!**

items.hint: Present tense of *leren* (to learn), 1st person singular.

items.prompt: Which opening sounds **more formal** when you talk to a stranger or in a shop?
items.feedbackCorrect: Right — **u** + verb form (*Kunt u…*) is the usual polite pattern.
items.feedbackIncorrect: **Kunt u** with *u* is more formal; **Kun je** is fine with friends.

items.prompt: In Dutch, yes/no questions often put the **verb before the subject** (e.g. *Ga je mee?*).
items.feedbackCorrect: Correct — verb-first order is typical for yes/no questions.
items.feedbackIncorrect: This is **true**: many yes/no questions start with the verb.

items.prompt: Say or write **one new sentence** using **OV-chipkaart**.
items.exampleAnswer: Example: *Ik wil graag OV-chipkaart met mijn nieuwe buren.* (adjust to your situation.)

steps.activity:
**Nice work.**

Next in this unit: **Real-life task · Fix a polite message—real stakes** — we keep building **Transport & city** with a new focus.

**2-minute revision:** skim the phrase bank from step 6 once more.

When you're ready, open **Quiz** for a compact recap — or **Flashcards** for the lemmas.

**Dutch culture insight**

**OV-chipkaart** and contactless check-in/out are standard; forgetting *uitchecken* can cost extra. *Vertraging* announcements are part of life; *De trein rijdt niet* is worth recognising by ear.

**Phrases — OV & delays**

| Dutch | English |
|-------|--------|
| *Excuseer, welke tram gaat naar …?* | Excuse me, which tram goes to …? |
| *Ik moet overstappen bij …* | I have to transfer at … |
| *Er is vertraging door werkzaamheden.* | There is a delay due to engineering works. |

**Mini dialogue**

**Reiziger:** Hoe laat vertrekt de volgende bus naar het ziekenhuis?
**Medewerker:** Over zes minuten, halte twee. Vergeet niet in te checken.

**Say or write:** Say you are late and will send an app using *Sorry, ik heb vertraging*.

*Optional reflection:* In one English sentence, what feels similar or different where you live?
steps.teacher_notes: Link to next lesson id in CMS when wired.

quiz_ideas: Self-check: choose the best reply to "Hoe gaat het?" (see step 7 in the lesson).
Gap-fill idea: write a sentence with **OV-chipkaart** in the present tense.
True/false: in Dutch yes/no questions the verb often comes before the subject.
Say one new polite request starting with **Kunt u …** or **Mag ik …**.
Paraphrase one Dutch line from the lesson input in your own English.
assessment.success_criteria: You can complete the step-7 self-check comfortably and produce at least one clear Dutch sentence about the lesson theme (spoken or written).

content_outline.speaking_frames_nl: Rol A/B dialogues (text-first). Gebruik frames: A: Ik heb last van … B: Dan moet je misschien … | A: Moet ik een afspraak maken? B: Ja, bel …
